subject: Sociology
title: Individual vs Structural Racism
description: Learn Individual vs Structural Racism in one minute.

hook.text: A landlord rejects a Black tenant. That's individual racism. A lending system that denies Black mortgages is structural.

buildup.text: Individual racism is personal prejudice. Structural racism is embedded in institutions, policies, and systems.

discovery.text: Structural racism needs no racist individuals. Neutral rules produce unequal results from unequal starting points.

twist.text: Most people focus on individual bias. Sociology shows that systems, not just attitudes, drive racial inequality.

climax.text: You can eliminate personal prejudice and still have racist outcomes if the structures remain unchanged.

punchline.text: Racism doesn't need racists. It just needs unchanged systems.

quiz.question: What is structural racism?
options: Racism embedded in institutions and policies
